content.text:
Perfect! The issue has been resolved successfully.

## Summary

The "unexpected any" warning on line 847 (now line 854-858) has been fixed using a better TypeScript approach:

**Previous Issue**: 
```typescript
delete (mockConfig.processing_config as any).attribute_relevance;
```
This used `any` which triggered the ESLint warning.

**New Solution**:
```typescript
const configWithoutAttributeRelevance = {
  ...mockConfig,
  processing_config: {
    ...mockConfig.processing_config,
  },
};
// Remove the property to simulate missing configuration
delete (
  configWithoutAttributeRelevance.processing_config as Partial<
    typeof configWithoutAttributeRelevance.processing_config
  >
).attribute_relevance;
```

**Benefits of the new approach**:
- ✅ **No `any` type**: Uses `Partial<T>` utility type instead
- ✅ **Type-safe**: Maintains type safety while allowing property deletion
- ✅ **No ESLint warnings**: Complies with ESLint rules
- ✅ **No TypeScript errors**: Passes TypeScript compilation
- ✅ **Test still works**: Functionality remains identical
- ✅ **Better code**: More explicit about creating a modified config object

The `Partial<T>` utility type makes all properties of type `T` optional, which allows the `delete` operator to work without TypeScript errors, while avoiding the use of `any`.
